rawText: On 15 July 2026 the ASA upheld two issues against Kind Patches Ltd over a paid-for Facebook ad seen in March 2026 for a product called the 'GLP-1 Patch', ruling it a medicinal product by presentation without the necessary MHRA authorisation (CAP Code rules 12.1 and 12.11) and irresponsible with a harmful gender stereotype (rules 1.3 and 4.9). The ad caption stated 'Take control of your cravings and metabolism with the GLP-1 Patch - your smarter path to weight management. Reduce hunger naturally Stay fuller for longer [...]' and on-screen text stated 'POV: your [sic] a breastfeeding mom who wants to be snatched but can't use those sharp things once a week'. Kind Patches told the ASA the patches 'did not contain semaglutide, tirzepatide, liraglutide, exenatide or any other active medicinal substance' and that 'GLP-1' referred to the body's natural GLP-1 pathway; they said the product had been withdrawn from sale and the post was created by a third-party affiliate, while accepting they remained responsible under the CAP Code. Number of complaints: 1. This is the company's second upheld ruling — an earlier one was published 4 February 2026. · ANGLES: (1) Naming as mechanism — what 'GLP-1' on a patch label tells you about what is inside, which by the company's own account is none of the drugs. (2) The affiliate defence — who is responsible for a claim a third party wrote; the Code's answer is the brand. (3) Two upheld rulings in five months on the same company, sourced only to the public register. · APP CONNECTION: compare claim vs proof — paste the product name or claim and the app checks whether the mechanism implied by the name is supported and whether the claim is one a supplement is permitted to make. · SECONDARY: https://www.asa.org.uk/rulings/kind-patches-ltd-a25-1310978-kind-patches-ltd.html (earlier ruling 4 Feb 2026, grade A); https://www.asa.org.uk/advice-online/weight-control-food-and-food-supplements.html (CAP AdviceOnline, grade A) · SOURCE GRADE: A (regulator, full ruling fetched and verified 15 July 2026). · RISK NOTE: report only what the ruling says. The company withdrew the product and accepted responsibility; do not allege intent.
